pr.title: Melee decomp: Ground, Kongo, and Zebes stage matches
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Exact-match decompilation slice for Ground manager plus Kongo and Zebes stage files. The PR body reports 6 newly exact functions across 3 files, 11 additional still-unmatched function improvements, clean isolated and full ship-set regression reports, and no header churn. Review centered on narrow matching tactics: a scoped Ground pragma, a Zebes inline wrapper that preserves an HSD_LObjSetColor call path, Kongo __FILE__/jobj inline cleanup, and static ownership for a local Kongo data object.

key_files.role: Largest stage-local decomp matching file; diff shows Kongo control-flow/local-name cleanup, helper refactors, assert macro use, typed pair-list data, PAD_STACK/volatile stack-shape tactics, and reviewer comments on file-name overrides, static data ownership, and jobj helper usage.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grkongo.c hunks around random_adder_f, grKongo_801D577C, grKongo_801D828C, grKongo_801D651C, grKongo_801D6668

key_files.role: Ground manager matching work; PR comments identify a new scoped global_optimizer pragma around Ground_801C5878 and report the function reaching 100% with no unit regression entry.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists Ground_801C0C2C as a new match and multiple Ground improvements

key_files.role: Zebes stage matching work; review discussed replacing a direct HSD_LObjSetColor call with an inline wrapper and concluded the wrapper still bottoms out in the same HSD helper and is a line-wrapper/codegen tactic.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists grZebes_801D9798 as a new match

what_changed.text: PR body reports exact-match decompilation for Ground manager plus Kongo/Zebes stage files, adding 6 newly exact functions across 3 files and improving 11 still-unmatched functions.
Changed files were limited to src/melee/gr/grkongo.c, src/melee/gr/ground.c, and src/melee/gr/grzebes.c.

Kongo changes rewrote several generated-looking locals and branches into clearer local names and structured switch/case blocks in grKongo_801D577C, including gp, jobj, angular_vel, limit_angle, angle_delta, abs_vel, spin_step, item_gobj, rand_val, and diff.
Kongo assert cleanup changed a manual __assert on gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep into HSD_ASSERTMSG using a small TaruKeepMsg helper.
Kongo data representation for grKg_803B7FD4 changed from a flat s32 array into a typed pair-list struct, with review feedback asking that the data object be static rather than extern.
Ground added a scoped pragma block; author follow-up describes it as global_optimizer off wrapped in push/pop around only Ground_801C5878.
Zebes replaced a direct HSD_LObjSetColor call site with grZebes_801DA254_inline2; follow-up says the wrapper still calls HSD_LObjSetColor and was used as a narrow codegen/stack-shape tactic.

smart_moves.text: The PR body included narrow verification details: baseline hash, changes_all command, slice regression report, full ship-set regression report, git diff --check, and review_lint summary.
The author explicitly bounded the Ground pragma exception by noting push/pop scope around only Ground_801C5878 and no leakage into Ground_801C58E0 or later code.
The Zebes wrapper concern was checked against behavior: follow-up states the wrapper bottoms out in HSD_LObjSetColor(lobj, color), preserving source-level operation and runtime call target.
Kongo locals were renamed away from temp_rXX/temp_fXX into role names where the diff made local roles apparent, improving reviewability without claiming new global semantics.

decomp_lessons.text: Scoped pragma exceptions can be discussed as matching tactics only when the PR records tight push/pop scope and match/regression evidence; this PR still had reviewer disagreement, so do not treat the tactic as generally preferred.
An inline wrapper around an HSD helper may be a narrow codegen/stack-shape tactic if review evidence shows it still calls the same HSD helper and does not remove the operation.
For stage-local data used only within the TU, reviewer feedback favored static ownership over an extern declaration.
Manual __FILE__ overrides inside a file can be review noise when they duplicate the file identity; reviewer requested removing a grkongo.c __FILE__ override and related mention.

naming_conventions.text: Use short role names for obvious local object pointers in stage code, such as gp for Ground*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, and item_gobj/kept_gobj for HSD_GObj* item references.

Replace temp_f/temp_r decompiler locals with local role names when the math or state-machine role is visible, as with angular_vel, limit_angle, angle_delta, abs_vel, spin_step, rand_val, diff, step, dx, dy, dz, dist, and length.
Keep unproven data-symbol names address-style; this PR retained grKg_ address-style names for Kongo data while adding only local struct/type names around their observed layout.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Scoped #pragma push/pop with global_optimizer off was used for Ground_801C5878 according to author follow-up; match evidence cited was 75.88% to 100.00% and no unit regression entry.
Zebes used a line/inline wrapper to affect inlining or stack shape while preserving HSD_LObjSetColor underneath.
Kongo grKongo_801D651C added PAD_STACK(8) while changing a flat list into copied typed pairs; grKongo_801D6668 used PAD_STACK(12), a volatile f32 length, and explicit frsqrte refinement math.
Kongo tau_range changed from returning a value to mutating a pointer, showing pointer-shaped helper refactoring as a matching tactic for local codegen.

review_feedback.text: Initial concern: replacing direct HSD_LObjSetColor with grZebes_801DA254_inline2 might regress use of HSD. Follow-up said the wrapper still bottoms out in HSD_LObjSetColor, and reviewer later said it was fine as a line wrapper to prevent inlining.

New pragma was flagged. Author defended it as a tightly scoped global_optimizer off around Ground_801C5878 with match evidence, but reviewer also commented 'bad, remove it'. The slice evidence does not show final resolution beyond merged PR metadata.
Reviewer requested removing a __FILE__ override because the file was already called grkongo.c and also removing a grkongo.c mention up top.
Reviewer requested changing a new extern declaration for grKg_803B7FD4 typed pair-list data to static.

Reviewer noted that a hand-written GetTranslation inline with jobj.h asserts was 'a call to jobj's stuff', suggesting the operation should be understood as JObj API/header-inline behavior rather than stage-local logic.

follow_up_queries: PR2687 Ground_801C5878 global_optimizer off pragma final diff
PR2687 grKg_803B7FD4 extern static final state
PR2687 grKongo_801D6668 volatile length PAD_STACK frsqrte
stage grkongo __FILE__ assert filename override review

notes: PR identity, changed files, verification claims, and review comments are strongly evidenced by loaded PR text and metadata.
The raw diff is truncated, so file-wide final state and all hunks cannot be independently confirmed from the loaded excerpt.
Some review threads show disagreement or intermediate hunks; intake does not infer final source resolution unless directly shown by loaded diff or metadata.
Verification results are reported by the PR body and bot text; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The merged state is present in metadata, but acceptance of every reviewed tactic should not be inferred as a global standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Scoped pragma exceptions need tight scope and match evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2687, a new Ground global_optimizer-off pragma was discussed as acceptable by the author only because it was wrapped in push/pop around Ground_801C5878 and paired with reported 100% match/no-regression evidence; reviewer disagreement means this is evidence for cautious exception handling, not a general recommendation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Wrapper tactic can preserve HSD helper behavior
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2687, replacing a direct HSD_LObjSetColor call with grZebes_801DA254_inline2 was accepted in review context because follow-up stated the wrapper still bottoms out in HSD_LObjSetColor and reviewer characterized it as a line wrapper to prevent inlining.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stage-local recovered data should remain static when local
accepted_candidate_records.text: Review feedback on PR #2687 requested changing the recovered grKg_803B7FD4 pair-list declaration from extern to static, supporting a local-ownership preference for TU-local Kongo data.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify wrapper tactics that preserve canonical helper calls
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note that a narrow wrapper used for inlining or stack shape may be reviewable when evidence shows it still calls the same canonical helper, preserves source-level behavior, and has match/regression evidence. PR #2687 provides a Zebes example around grZebes_801DA254_inline2 and HSD_LObjSetColor.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards cover matching tactics and header helpers broadly; this PR gives a concrete reviewed example of a wrapper tactic that preserves a helper call.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify manual __FILE__ override review risk
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that manual __FILE__ overrides inside a TU should not duplicate the file's normal identity and should be justified by assert/header-line evidence. PR #2687 review requested removing a grkongo.c __FILE__ override because the file was already grkongo.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: The standards discuss assert/report macro cleanup and section/TU evidence; this review adds a concrete caution about redundant file-name overrides.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gr/ground.c pragma exception history
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2687 introduced a scoped pragma around Ground_801C5878 according to review comments, with author-cited match evidence and reviewer objection. If path facts track exceptional pragmas, record this as a reviewed historical exception candidate, not as a preferred style.
source_update_candidates.reason: Exceptional pragmas are high-risk and useful to track by path if present in final source, but the loaded slice alone does not prove final source state.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'global_optimizer off is acceptable' as a general rule; PR evidence shows it was controversial and defended only as a narrow exception.
Do not infer the final resolution of all review comments from merged state alone; the raw diff is truncated and review hunks may be intermediate.
Do not promote static float literal extraction in grkongo.c as a lesson; the diff shows named f32 constants, but the slice does not provide enough data-ownership rationale.
Do not claim independent verification beyond the PR-reported build/regression results.
